purpose: Land T1 artifacts so the operator can run one pod-based OpenAI-compatible endpoint proof without any new agent spend during this session.
rationale: Actually creating a RunPod pod and waiting 10-30 minutes for vLLM to download and serve gpt-oss-120b is a human-operator action: it spends ~$2/h and requires interactive monitoring of download/startup logs. This session delivers the full driver, profile, and runbook so the operator can run the proof with one command and capture an audit.

code_changes.change: Added optional docker_args field to RunpodProfile; parsed in parse_profile (follow-up path accepts; template path rejects); emitted as dockerArgs in _build_create_body.
code_changes.reason: Current profile shape cannot express a vLLM-serving pod because it has no container command path; template_id-based profiles are baked upstream, and we do not yet own a vLLM template.

artifacts_landed.role: canonical example profile for T1 proof
operator driver: create pod, poll /models, stop on success, emit audit
operator runbook: preconditions, procedure, kill criteria, continue criteria

kill_criteria_encoded_in_runbook: Cannot schedule H100 80GB in SECURE within 15 minutes
vLLM cannot load gpt-oss-120b within max-wait-minutes
Accumulated spend crosses $4.00 without verdict: usable
continue_criteria_encoded_in_runbook: verdict: usable recorded in T1 audit
GET /api/v1/runpod/heavy-lane/health returns status: usable against the live pod
audit captures pod_id, hourly_cost_usd, first-token latency, explicit stop action
mutations_performed_this_session: repo docs: gpt-oss-120b profile YAML + runbook + driver script
next_tranche: T2 - Profile YAML catalog (can proceed in parallel with operator driving T1)
